gaps.gap: Tag stored as free-text string on the existing project record, or as a first-class Tag entity with its own table + relation to the project?
interpretations: Tag = free-text string field added to the existing project record; extend persistence contract CT2, no new table.
Tag = first-class entity with its own table + many-to-one/many relation to the project; freelancer manages a controlled vocabulary.
gaps.recommended_default: Tag = free-text string field added to the existing project record; extend persistence contract CT2, no new table.
gaps.reason: Two builds differ in data-model shape (scalar column on existing entity vs new Tag table + FK relationship) and in how the feature plugs into the C1 persistence seam (extend CT2 vs new contract). Data-model + seam fork = architecture.

gaps.gap: Does a project carry exactly one label, or multiple tags?
interpretations: Each project carries exactly one label (singular, as the CR words it).
gaps.recommended_default: Each project carries exactly one label (singular, as the CR words it).
gaps.reason: CR says 'a label' (singular); the live fork is the optional additional capability of multi-tag riding on the same surface. In/out of multi-tag, structure unchanged either way = scope.

gaps.gap: Does filtering accept a single tag, or multiple tags combined (AND/OR)?
interpretations: Filter the project list by a single tag at a time.
Filter by multiple tags combined with AND/OR semantics.
gaps.recommended_default: Filter the project list by a single tag at a time.
gaps.reason: Same query surface either way; what moves is how much filtering capability ships (single vs combinatorial). Pure in/out of capability = scope, not architecture.
